summary: Phase 4 Month-End Closing testing complete. Added 10 new pytest cases (all pass, total 38/38). Backend coverage: list endpoint auth, preview opening_date (Feb->Mar, Dec->Jan rollover), invalid period 400, non-admin 403 on run, run+list+already-exists 400+undo+404 with safe period 2025-01, and full end-to-end with seed entry (party with 10g jama in 2024-06 -> verify Opening Balance entry created on 2024-07-01 with type='jama', gold=10 positive, remarks='Carried forward from 2024-06', then undo deletes it). Frontend: Month-End tab [tab-closings] renders, preview button shows table, Run Closing correctly disabled when 0 parties, no regression on other tabs. Could not perform full UI run+undo flow because the live DB had 0 parties with non-zero balance up to 2026-02 (the seed parties Kishore Bhai/Mahendra Jewels/Sunder Bullion mentioned in the task are not present in this env — DB appears reset). End-to-end run/undo IS fully covered at the API layer.

minor.issue: Does NOT expose 'is_opening' and 'closing_period' fields in the response, although they ARE stored in MongoDB. Frontend therefore cannot visually distinguish/highlight opening entries (e.g., add a badge or prevent edit). Running balance still works because the entries exist as normal entries. Consider adding these fields to serialize_entry.

action_items: (Optional, minor) Add 'is_opening' and 'closing_period' to serialize_entry() in server.py so frontend can mark/highlight opening entries on Party Ledger as per the Phase-4 spec ('regular entry tagged is_opening=true').
(Optional) If desired, prevent edit/delete of opening entries from user role (currently they look like normal entries to the UI).

(Informational) Seed parties (Kishore Bhai, Mahendra Jewels, Sunder Bullion) referenced in the task description are NOT present in the live preview DB — only 'GVS (Piyush Shroff)' exists with a balance dated 12 June 2026. If main agent wants visual UI verification of run+undo on the live env, please run a quick seed.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /closings/run correctly stores absolute values with sign encoded via type (jama/naame), avoiding negative-value confusion in the ledger.
Sign-determination logic in /closings/run uses amount first, falls back to gold > fine_gold > silver — matches preview logic. Good.
Closings store entry_ids[] making undo a clean targeted delete (no stale orphan entries). Good design.
Closings.jsx uses thisMonthMinusOne() as default period — sensible UX.
Closings.jsx correctly hides Run Closing button for non-admin (isAdmin check) and disables it when preview is null / already_run / no parties.
MINOR: Closings.jsx uses window.confirm() for undo — works but inconsistent with the modal-based confirmation used for Run. Consider replacing with a Dialog for visual consistency.
MINOR: serialize_entry() is missing is_opening/closing_period — opening entries become indistinguishable from manual entries to the frontend.

success_rate.frontend: Partial — UI elements verified; full run+undo flow not executed in UI due to empty live DB

seed_data_creation: No persistent seed. test_closings_run_creates_opening_entries_with_flag creates a TEST_ClosePty_<hex> party + 1 entry in 2024-06, runs+undoes closing, then cleans up everything.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 4 backend fully verified via pytest (38/38). Closings testids: closings-tab, closing-period-input, closing-preview-button, closing-run-button, closing-confirm-run, closing-preview-table, closings-history-table, closing-undo-<period>. For UI run flow, ensure DB has at least one party with entries dated within the target period — otherwise Run button is correctly disabled. Safe period for fresh runs: 2025-01 (no production data — created=0, skipped=0). Backend tests auto-cleanup, no DB pollution. Known minor: GET /api/entries serializer drops is_opening + closing_period.
rca of the issue: No critical bug found. One minor improvement: serialize_entry omits is_opening/closing_period fields, so the frontend treats opening entries as regular entries. Fix: add these two fields to serialize_entry() in /app/backend/server.py at lines 163-180.
